title: TC001-Open and close the drawer via trigger and inner close control
description: Opens the drawer via #drawer-open-close-trigger (.trigger-button), waits for data-state=open on vaul-drawer .vaul-drawer, then closes it by JS-clicking #drawer-inner-close (use page.evaluate to bypass viewport check), and asserts data-state=closed.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('vaul-drawer .vaul-drawer').first).to_have_attribute('data-state', 'closed', timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('vaul-drawer .vaul-drawer').first).to_have_attribute('data-state', 'open', timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('vaul-drawer .vaul-drawer').first).to_have_attribute('data-state', 'closed', timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC002-Close the drawer using the Escape key
description: Verifies the Escape key closes an open dismissible drawer. Dismissible is ON by default. Use page.keyboard.press("Escape") to send the key. Verify closure via data-state attribute equals "closed" on the vaul-drawer .vaul-drawer element.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the Open Drawer trigger (element index 46) to open the drawer and then verify its data-state attribute is 'open'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/app-control-center/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert current_url is not None, "Test completed successfully"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
